editor_settings.title: Canon PowerShot G7 X Mark III Price Comparison May 2026: MSRP vs Used vs Refurbished
editor_settings.content:
Canon PowerShot G7 X Mark III Price Comparison May 2026: MSRP vs Used vs Refurbished

The Canon PowerShot G7 X Mark III has one official price.

But that is not the price most people are seeing.

In May 2026, the real price depends on one thing: whether you can actually find stock before everyone else does.

Quick Price Breakdown

Here is the clean version:

  • New MSRP: about $849.99
  • New market price: about $1,175 to $1,299
  • Canon refurbished: about $679.99 to $703.99
  • Used or open box: about $750 to $950
  • 30th Anniversary Graphite Edition: $1,299 retail, often higher on resale

That is a big spread for one compact camera.

And honestly, the new market price is the least attractive part of the list.

MSRP Is The Dream Price

The normal retail price sits around $849.99.

You might see it at Canon, B&H, Adorama, Best Buy, or another authorized dealer.

If it is actually in stock at that price, that is one of the better new-camera buys right now.

The problem is that the G7 X Mark III is still riding the creator-camera wave. Stock appears, gets posted online, and disappears quickly.

The Reseller Price Is Painful

The realistic available price is often closer to $1,175 to $1,299.

That is usually from third-party marketplace sellers, bundles, or listings where the camera is being priced like a scarce collectible instead of a compact camera from 2019.

I would be careful here.

At $1,200, the G7 X Mark III is no longer a simple fun camera purchase. You are paying hype tax.

Refurbished Is The Best Value

The refurbished price is where the camera still makes the most sense.

Canon refurbished units have been seen around $679.99, and some listings land closer to $703.99.

That is the sweet spot.

The catch is speed. These deals do not sit around like normal stock. If the price is real and the seller is trustworthy, waiting usually means losing it.

Used And Open Box Are In The Middle

Used and open-box prices are usually around $750 to $950.

That range can be fair, but condition matters more than the number.

A $775 unit with a clean lens, good battery, charger, SD card, and return policy can be better than a $700 mystery listing with bad photos.

For this camera, clean condition is worth paying for.

One Deal Worth Checking

If you are comparing prices, check smaller product listings before accepting marketplace markup.

Sometimes the better buy is not the loudest listing.

[product-card:canon-powershot-g7-x-mark-iii-201mp-42x-zoom-black-vlogging-camera-with-wi-fi-touchscreen]


Best Choice By Buyer Type

  • Best new buy: MSRP around $849.99
  • Best overall value: refurbished under $704
  • Best realistic used buy: excellent condition under $850
  • Worst buy: random marketplace bundle over $1,200

My honest take: do not panic-buy this camera just because it is viral.

If you can get it at MSRP or refurbished pricing, great. If every listing is over $1,100, wait, set alerts, or look at the Sony ZV-1 II or Canon PowerShot V10.

The G7 X Mark III is good.

It is not magic at any price.
